title: ArCoPresenceRequirement
description: A15-SE2 (aristotelian-sketch-04 / dialect-compilation-surface-sketch-01 DCS1 side 1). Hard constraint: the named characters must co-locate at >= min_count substrate events whose ids fall within the named phase's scope_event_ids. Compiler stage-1 extracts to per-event STRIPS preconditions; stage-3 planner generates intermediate events to satisfy them when authored substrate doesn't already do so. A7.13 verifies structural integrity at this layer (>= 2 character refs; characters resolve in mythos; phase resolves in mythos; min_count >= 1); whether the substrate actually carries the co-presence is a compiler-stage-3 question, not a Tier-2 check (DOQ-AR4-1). Records live on ArMythos.co_presence_requirements; the dialect does not track them at encoding scope. PFS-N1..N4.

id.description: Stable identifier for this requirement (e.g., 'copres_hamlet_claudius_end'). PFS-N1.

character_ref_ids.description: Character ids participating in the co-presence requirement. minItems 2 per A15-SE2 — a co-presence requirement names at least two characters. A7.13 check 2 separately enforces that all ids resolve to ArCharacter records in the enclosing mythos's `characters` tuple; the schema enforces cardinality at shape time. Order is author-preserved but not semantically meaningful. Plain-string references per PFS6-X2. PFS-N2.

phase_id.description: ArPhase id naming the phase whose scope_event_ids window the requirement applies over. A7.13 check 3 separately enforces that the phase resolves in the enclosing mythos's `phases` tuple. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS-N3.

min_count.description: Minimum number of substrate events within the named phase at which all named characters must be co-located. Default at the Python layer is 1. Schema enforces >= 1 (A7.13 check 4 is the second line of defense for Python-level zero values that bypass the schema). The compiler at stage 3 plans events to satisfy this count; a count exceeding what the planner can satisfy is a CS6 (compilation-sketch-01) infeasibility. PFS-N4.
